bullets: Sabic re-bid June PP down up to 300: yarn 1,200, copo 1,230, random 1,600 CIF.
NSRP 114G to 35,500-35,800; merchants converge near lows.
Jinneng FOB ~1,200; some signed at 1,185 FOB China.
Sabic HDPE Film finalized 1,320 (F04660 1,550); blow B5428 1,420.
Qamar F1 at 39,500; Chinese oil-based LLDPE to 34,700.
Shintech SE950/SE1000 cut to 24,500-25,000 to push goods.
AGC/TPC Vina hold June offers, focus on closing orders.

editorial.changedNote: Broad declines across PP and PE on Sabic re-bid

summary: Sabic and Chinese makers cut HDPE/LLDPE finals and offers, yet cautious buyers bid still lower.
Domestic HDPE/LDPE/LLDPE fell broadly on a coming wave of Chinese imports and weak demand.
Regional indices showed sharp NEA/SEA PE declines, leaving Vietnam among the lowest.
paras: Sabic finalized late-June HDPE ex-Singapore: common HDPE Film 1,320 (80 below offer), F04660 1,550 (120 above offer), blow B5428 1,420, B5429 1,360, injection M200056 1,600 (100 above offer). LDPE/LLDPE finals not yet out.
A northern source cited Qamar HDPE F1 at 1,290 and JG Summit HDPE Film at 1,275 (ex-China), pending verification.
Chinese oil-based LLDPE 7042/2020F at 1,155 FOB Qinzhou/Nansha, ~1,175 CIF Hai Phong; coal-based 7042 ~1,130-1,160 CIF. STL HDPE Film FS7000 to 1,300-1,310 (down 40); buyers still bid lower.
Domestically, Qamar F1 finalized at 39,500 (Hung Yen), the cheapest; Chinese/US/Russian HDPE Film traded 38,000-39,000.
LDPE: premium Sabic/Borouge/Tasnee/Lotte Titan/PTT at 49,000-50,000; US-origin 47,000-48,000. LLDPE cheapest from China - oil-based 7042/7042N to 34,700 for late-June.

LSP held LLDPE Film 38,300 South / 39,000 North; HDPE Film 41,000-41,200 South / 41,900 North - down sharply. Regional: NEA HDPE -25, LLDPE -15, LDPE -80; SEA HDPE -25 to 1,340-1,350, LLDPE -55 to 1,300, mC6 -55 to 1,450, LDPE -45 to 1,550.

summary: Asian PP fell on weak demand and rising Chinese stocks; Sabic and Jinneng cut bids sharply.
Vietnam shifted into oversupply, forcing distributors to lower local and imported PP.
LSP and Hyosung held official offers while merchants undercut them.
paras: Per Platts on May 28, NEA PP yarn/injection/BOPP flat, IPP Film -10, block copo -50. SEA PP all -20: yarn/injection to 1,270, BOPP 1,290, IPP/block copo 1,320 (Vietnam ~40-50 below SEA).
PP fell across Asia on weak oil and sentiment; Chinese stocks rose and shipping costs hurt exports. SEA activity thinned ahead of Hari Raya Haji.

Earlier this week Sabic offered late-June PP very high (homo 1,500, copo 1,540-1,550, random 1,640). On May 28 Sabic re-bid: yarn/injection 1,200 (down 300), copo 1,230 (down 310-320), random 1,600 (down 40) - a shock that deepened pressure.
Jinneng FOB China: yarn/injection HP550J/HP500N 1,200, BOPP 1,210; one distributor at 1,220-1,230 CIF, some buyers signed at 1,185 FOB China.

Domestic offers drifted sideways to soft. A major distributor offered NSRP 114G at 35,800 to big buyers; another signed 200 tons at 35,500; smaller firms to 35,600. BSR T3034 to 36,000-36,800, distant delivery slightly above 35,000.

Chinese PP yarn/injection to 35,000 North and South - near market low; copo below 40,000 for some grades, off-grade copo 35,000-35,500. LSP held PP homo 38,300 South / 39,000 North, copo 39,000-39,200 South / 39,900 North; Hyosung yarn ~37,400 with VAT, copo 39,600 ex-VAT.

summary: SEA buyers avoided large purchases, consuming heavy earlier-hoarded stock.
Domestic sellers cut American and Chinese imports while local makers held June offers.
Per Platts, China PVC fell 70 to 860 CFR and SEA eased 10 to 810 CFR on weak demand.
paras: Per Platts, China PVC -70 to 860 CFR; SEA -10 to 810 CFR. Asian PVC kept falling. China inventory high, demand weak, supply abundant; run rate ~69.85% (up 0.45 pt).
SEA buyers, holding earlier-hoarded stock, bought small.
In Vietnam, sellers cut Shintech SE950/SE1000 to 24,500-25,000, a deep drop; buyers turned cautious.
Northern sellers cut Chinese SG-5 to 22,000-22,100 to compete; Chinese oil-based offers to 24,300-24,500. Trading stayed slow.
AGC Vina and TPC Vina held June SP660/SG660/PM66R at 22,800-23,100 ex-VAT (~24,624-24,948 with VAT), focusing on closing orders.
